error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to Hermes API Server
error.endpoint_not_found: Hermes API endpoint not found. Use the server base URL without /v1.
error.ssl_error: TLS certificate validation failed. Use a trusted certificate or disable SSL verification for this local endpoint.

user.title: Home Assistant Hermes Conversation
user.description: Connect Home Assistant Assist to a Hermes Agent API Server.

data_description.url: Use https://homeassistant.local:8443 for the Hermes add-on proxy. Server base URL only; do not include /v1.
data_description.verify_ssl: Off by default for the local Home Assistant HTTPS proxy/self-signed certificate.
data_description.prompt: System instructions sent to Hermes for each Assist request. Templates are supported. Keep the JSON action instruction if you want native HA service execution.
data_description.allow_control: When enabled, Hermes may return structured JSON actions and this integration will validate and execute them with hass.services.async_call.
data_description.allowed_domains: Comma-separated domains Hermes may change, for example: light,switch,script. Empty means no domain restriction.
data_description.allowed_entities: Optional comma-separated entity allowlist. Leave empty to allow any entity in the allowed domains.
data_description.confirm_domains: Comma-separated domains that must be confirmed by the next voice turn before execution.
data_description.confirm_services: Comma-separated services that must be confirmed by the next voice turn before execution.

data_description.prompt: For native HA execution, instruct Hermes to return JSON actions for changes and answer normally for status queries.
data_description.allow_control: If disabled, structured actions are rejected and Hermes can only answer through text.
data_description.allowed_domains: Comma-separated domains that may be changed through native service calls.
